Story Title: Remove binary yes/no checkpoint UI
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- All checkbox / yes/no toggle controls removed from macro checkpoint cards
- Checkpoint cards display name, type badge, reached_at, and evidence count only
- No regression on checkpoint completion flow (completion still works via existing PATCH endpoint)
- UI passes lint and type-check
Notes: Quick credibility win with Qasim. No backend change.

Story Title: Remove manual evidence upload flow
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Evidence attachment modal removed from the project detail UI
- POST /api/projects/{id}/checkpoints/{id}/evidence endpoint disabled (returns 410 Gone)
- gate_evidence rows are written only by automated paths (AI analysis, Avoma fetch, system events)
- Existing evidence rows are retained in DB — not deleted
- All related frontend components (EvidenceModal, FileUpload, UploadProgressBar) removed from bundle
Notes: Pairs with governance-as-layer model change. Do together.

Story Title: Hide micro gates from project detail UI
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- micro_artifacts section no longer rendered on project detail page
- Micro artifact data still returned by GET /api/projects/{id}/gates (backend unchanged)
- Phase progression visual still shows phase_complete correctly
- No micro artifact rows are deleted from DB
- Audit trail / timeline still surfaces micro events
Notes: Data stays, UI hides it. Audit trail unaffected.

Story Title: Remove micro/macro ownership split from data model and UI labels
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- 'Micro gate' and 'Macro gate' labels removed from all UI text, tooltips, and badge copy
- casdm_config config_type values unified — no UI or API surfaces the micro/macro split as a distinct class
- API responses no longer include a top-level micro_artifacts vs macro_checkpoints split in the gate status response; all items returned as a flat checkpoints[] array sorted by phase + item_order
- Existing config_type column retained in DB for internal routing only
Notes: Single ownership model. Backend routing preserved internally.

Story Title: V005 — Batched schema migration (project fields + weekly_status_logs fields)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- V005__phase3_additions.sql applies cleanly on top of V004 schema
- projects table gains nullable columns: health_status TEXT CHECK IN ('red','amber','green'), actual_kickoff_date DATE, actual_customer_kickoff_date DATE, implementation_lead TEXT, reporter TEXT, assignee TEXT, priority TEXT CHECK IN ('low','medium','high','critical'), resolution TEXT CHECK IN ('unresolved','resolved','cancelled'), request_type TEXT
- weekly_status_logs table gains: completed_this_week TEXT, planned_next_week TEXT
- All new columns are nullable with no DEFAULT to avoid breaking existing rows
- New indexes: idx_projects_health_status, idx_projects_priority
- Migration is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS / DO $$ guards where applicable)
- Migration runs cleanly on a copy of production schema before merging
Notes: Foundational. All Phase 3 features depend on these columns existing.

Story Title: V005b — project_decisions table
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New table project_decisions: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), project_id UUID NOT NULL FK projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, decision_url TEXT, raised_by TEXT NOT NULL, raised_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), approver TEXT, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK IN ('open','approved','rejected') DEFAULT 'open', resolution_notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
- Index: idx_project_decisions_project_id ON project_decisions(project_id)
- Index: idx_project_decisions_status ON project_decisions(status)
- kiro_phase2 runtime role granted INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE on project_decisions and its sequence
- Migration idempotent
Notes: Decisions table. No foreign key to users — store names as TEXT consistent with existing pattern.

Story Title: Governance data model — replace artifact storage with source references + scores
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- gate_evidence table: file_content, file_blob, and any raw transcript TEXT columns dropped from schema (V005c migration)
- gate_evidence retains: id, checkpoint_id, evidence_type, value (URL/S3 key/reference), label, link_metadata JSONB, score NUMERIC(4,2), score_detail JSONB, uploaded_by, created_at
- score and score_detail columns added to gate_evidence if not present
- No Lambda reads raw content from gate_evidence — only reference values
- All existing gate_evidence rows migrated: any raw content moved to S3 reference; DB row updated with s3_key
- Unit tests confirm no handler imports or writes blob content
Notes: Foundation for quality scoring. Pairs with CHG-04.

Story Title: Quality scoring chain — Bedrock scores gates; low-confidence gates blocked
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/analysis/{projectId}/{checkpointId}/score triggers Bedrock scoring for a checkpoint
- Reads transcript/document reference from gate_evidence (S3 key or Avoma URL)
- Reads prompt from analysis_prompts for the checkpoint_name; falls back to generic if none
- Invokes Bedrock AgentCore; parses response: {topics_covered[], topics_missing[], passed: boolean, confidence: number 0-1}
- Writes score and score_detail to gate_evidence row
- If confidence < 0.6 AND passed=false: macro_checkpoints.reached_at is NOT set; returns {blocked: true, reason: 'Low confidence score', score}
- If confidence >= 0.6 OR passed=true: checkpoint completion proceeds normally
- Threshold configurable via SSM parameter /deliverpro/config/score-threshold
- Returns 502 AGENT_UNAVAILABLE on AgentCore failure; gate is NOT blocked on infra failure (fail-open)
Notes: Strict order: CHG-03 (data model) → this story → CHG-05 (enforcement).

Story Title: Gate enforcement — phase transition blocked until mandatory checkpoints pass scoring
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/projects/{id}/gates response includes per-checkpoint: blocked_reason TEXT when a gate has score but confidence < threshold
- POST /api/projects/{id}/advance-phase returns 400 {code: 'PHASE_BLOCKED', missing: string[], blocked: string[]} if any mandatory checkpoint in current phase has reached_at IS NULL or blocked_reason IS NOT NULL
- Frontend phase progression visual shows a lock icon on any checkpoint with blocked_reason
- Leadership/admin role can override a blocked gate via PATCH /api/projects/{id}/checkpoints/{id}/override with required {override_reason: string}
- Override writes to gate_evidence with evidence_type='override' and the reason
- All override events emitted to governance_events table
Notes: Leadership override keeps the platform usable without bypassing accountability.

Story Title: Automated project init — provision Slack channels on project creation
Source: Impact Report — CHANGE ⚠️ Watch item (Slack portion)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- On POST /api/projects, after DB row created, Lambda invokes Slack provisioning step
- Uses provisioning credential (channels:read + channels:manage) from SSM
- Calls conversations.list to check if channels named {jira_key}-micro and {jira_key}-macro already exist
- If not found: calls conversations.create to create both channels
- Stores resulting channel IDs in projects.slack_micro_channel_id and slack_macro_channel_id
- If Slack API fails: project creation succeeds anyway; channels marked as pending in a new projects.slack_provisioning_status TEXT column ('pending'/'provisioned'/'failed')
- Retry endpoint: POST /api/projects/{id}/provision-slack for manual retry by admin
- Bot token used only for chat:write at runtime — provisioning token never stored in DB
Notes: Provisioning is best-effort; project creation never blocked by Slack failure.

Story Title: Append-only audit history — deny UPDATE/DELETE on governance_events
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- PostgreSQL row-level trigger on governance_events BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE raises exception 'governance_events is append-only'
- Trigger function created in V005d migration and attached to table
- kiro_mcp role: UPDATE and DELETE privileges revoked on governance_events (verified post-migration)
- kiro_phase2 role: UPDATE and DELETE privileges revoked on governance_events
- Unit test: attempt to UPDATE a governance_events row via the application DB pool raises DB error
- Existing governance_events rows unaffected
Notes: Append-only enforcement at DB level — cannot be bypassed by application code.

Story Title: Projects API — expose new V005 fields (health_status, actual dates, implementation_lead, etc.)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/projects returns health_status, actual_kickoff_date, actual_customer_kickoff_date, implementation_lead, reporter, assignee, priority, resolution, request_type in ProjectListItem and ProjectDetail responses
- PATCH /api/projects/{id} accepts and validates all new fields: health_status CHECK enum, priority CHECK enum, resolution CHECK enum, dates as ISO strings
- Zod schema updated for all new fields
- specs/api/projects.yaml updated with new fields and enums
- Returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with field name for invalid enum values
- Shared TypeScript types in packages/shared/types/projects.ts updated
Notes: Expose the V005 columns through the API layer.

Story Title: Actual-date auto-stamp on checklist completion events
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- When onboarding checklist item named 'Internal Kickoff' is completed: projects.actual_kickoff_date stamped with completed_at (idempotent — only sets if currently NULL)
- When onboarding checklist item named 'Customer Kickoff' is completed: projects.actual_customer_kickoff_date stamped with completed_at (idempotent)
- Stamping is done inside the same transaction as checklist item completion
- If the project already has a non-NULL actual_kickoff_date, the stamp is skipped (no overwrite)
- Governance event emitted: {update_text: 'actual_kickoff_date auto-stamped', type: 'micro'}
- Unit tests cover: first stamp succeeds, second stamp is skipped
Notes: Event-driven, no manual entry needed.

Story Title: Weekly status log — expose completed_this_week + planned_next_week fields
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{id}/status-logs accepts completed_this_week TEXT (max 4000 chars) and planned_next_week TEXT (max 4000 chars) in request body
- Both fields optional; existing required fields unchanged
- GET /api/projects/{id}/status-logs response includes completed_this_week and planned_next_week per log entry
- Zod schema updated; specs/api/meetings.yaml updated
- Returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR if either field exceeds 4000 chars
Notes: Low-effort extension of existing endpoint.

Story Title: Decision ticket workflow — backend (create, approve/reject, list)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{id}/decisions creates a decision per project_decisions schema
- Accepts: {title, description, decision_url?, raised_by} — raised_by defaults to current user email
- Returns 201 with created decision; status defaults to 'open'
- PATCH /api/projects/{id}/decisions/{decisionId}/approve sets status='approved', approver=current user, approved_at=now()
- PATCH /api/projects/{id}/decisions/{decisionId}/reject accepts {resolution_notes} and sets status='rejected'
- Auth: all roles can create; leadership/admin only can approve/reject
- Returns 403 FORBIDDEN for role mismatch on approve/reject
- Returns 409 ALREADY_RESOLVED if decision status is not 'open'
- GET /api/projects/{id}/decisions returns all decisions sorted by raised_at DESC; supports filter: status
- On approve or reject: notify_slack called with macro channel, message: '[jira_key] Decision [title] [approved/rejected] by [actor]'
Notes: Replaces the current Jira decision-ticket workflow.

Story Title: RAG health status — backend PATCH + leadership dashboard surface
Notes: Distinct from burn rate. PM-owned signal.

Story Title: Friday status reminder — EventBridge cron + Slack notify
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- EventBridge rule triggers Lambda every Friday at 12:00 UTC
- Lambda queries projects where status='Active' and no weekly_status_logs entry exists for current ISO week (week number + year)
- For each such project: calls notify_slack with macro channel and message: '⚠️ [jira_key] — weekly status log missing for this week. Please update before end of day.'
- If project has no slack_macro_channel_id: skips silently, logs warning
- Maximum 50 projects processed per invocation; paginates if more
- Lambda execution logged to CloudWatch with count of reminded projects
- CloudWatch alarm: Lambda error rate > 0 on this function
Notes: Replaces Nayab's manual Friday chase.

Story Title: Resource-assignment notify — trigger on resource_assignment_date set
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- When PATCH /api/projects/{id} sets resource_assignment_date for the first time (currently NULL → non-NULL): Slack macro channel notified: '📋 [jira_key] — resources assigned. Phase 0 onboarding checklist is now active.'
- If slack_macro_channel_id is NULL at time of notify: skip silently, log warning
- Notification is idempotent — only fires once (first time resource_assignment_date is set from NULL)
- Governance event emitted: {update_text: 'resource_assignment_date set', type: 'micro'}
Notes: Single-fire event. Idempotent by NULL→non-NULL check.

Story Title: Decision ticket workflow — frontend (decisions tab, raise, approve/reject UI)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Decisions tab added to project detail page navigation
- Decisions list shows all decisions for the project: title, status badge, raised_by, raised_at, approver (if approved/rejected), decision_url (clickable link)
- 'Raise Decision' button opens modal with: title (required), description (textarea), decision_url (optional URL), raised_by (pre-filled with current user)
- On submit calls POST /api/projects/{id}/decisions
- Leadership/admin users see 'Approve' and 'Reject' buttons on open decisions
- Reject action opens inline textarea for resolution_notes before confirming
- On approve/reject calls appropriate PATCH endpoint
- Approved decisions show green badge; rejected show grey with resolution notes; open show amber
- Empty state: 'No decisions raised for this project'
Notes: Replaces Jira decision ticket workflow.

Story Title: RAG health status — frontend (project cards + detail + leadership dashboard)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Project list cards show a RAG dot (red/amber/green) next to the project title; grey if null
- Clicking the RAG dot on the project detail header opens an inline popover: current status, optional health_notes textarea, Save button
- Save calls PATCH /api/projects/{id}/health
- Leadership dashboard /dashboard shows projects_by_health as three count badges (red/amber/green counts)
- Stalled projects list on dashboard also shows their health_status dot
- CASL: pm, leadership, admin can change health; engineer/sa see it read-only
Notes: Visual health signal on every project.

Story Title: Weekly status log UI — add completed_this_week + planned_next_week fields
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- 'Log Status Call' form adds two new textareas: 'What we completed this week' (mapped to completed_this_week) and 'What's planned next week' (mapped to planned_next_week)
- Both fields optional, max 4000 chars with character counter
- Existing status log list cards show completed_this_week and planned_next_week sections when non-null
- Collapsed by default if empty; expanded if content present
Notes: Low-effort extension of existing form.

Story Title: IPR checkpoint type — config-driven gate with Ramesh-approval block
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- casdm_config gains a new config_type value 'ipr_checkpoint' (added to CHECK constraint in V005e)
- IPR checkpoint seeded in Phase 2 (Design & Review) for all project types
- Completing an IPR checkpoint requires: occurred=true AND approver field set (Ramesh or equivalent)
- PATCH /api/projects/{id}/checkpoints/{id} for ipr_checkpoint type: accepts {occurred: boolean, approver: string, meeting_date: string, result_detail?: string}
- phase_complete for Phase 2 returns false if the IPR checkpoint has reached_at IS NULL, regardless of other checkpoints
- Returns 400 APPROVER_REQUIRED if approver is empty on IPR completion attempt
- Seed data: one IPR checkpoint row in casdm_config for each project_type
Notes: Models the real 'cannot proceed to Build without Ramesh sign-off' rule.

Story Title: Avoma report reference store — fetch metadata + auto-match by project key (extends DP-37)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Extends the DP-37 Avoma fetch Lambda to also call GET /api/v1/meetings/{id}/report (Avoma report endpoint)
- Stores in gate_evidence: meeting_id (Avoma internal ID), report_url (canonical Avoma link), title, date, duration_minutes, ai_summary TEXT (max 2000 chars — summary only, not full transcript)
- Raw transcript content NEVER written to RDS; if S3 storage needed, key stored only
- Auto-match: if meeting title contains the project's jira_key (case-insensitive), system auto-associates the meeting to the project without manual input
- Auto-match runs as a background Lambda on Avoma webhook (new Avoma event subscription) or on-demand via POST /api/projects/{id}/avoma/sync
- ⚠️ HIPAA guard: if project.project_type contains 'health' or 'hipaa' flag (new projects.is_hipaa BOOLEAN column), Avoma summary is stored to S3 only; gate_evidence.ai_summary set to '[Content stored in S3 — HIPAA project]'
- BAA confirmation check: Lambda reads SSM /deliverpro/config/avoma-baa-confirmed; if 'false', Avoma summary storage is skipped entirely
Notes: Governance-as-layer redesign of Avoma integration. HIPAA guard mandatory.

Story Title: Avoma auto-match + IPR transcript quality score
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- When an Avoma meeting is auto-matched to an IPR checkpoint: quality scoring chain (CHG-04) is triggered automatically
- Score result stored in gate_evidence.score and score_detail
- IPR checkpoint result_detail updated with: 'Auto-scored from Avoma [meeting title] on [date]. Confidence: [score]. Topics covered: [list]. Topics missing: [list].'
- If confidence >= threshold: IPR checkpoint marked as occurred=true automatically; Slack macro channel notified
- If confidence < threshold: IPR checkpoint flagged as blocked_reason='Low meeting quality score — manual review required'
- Nayab (pm role) can still manually override via CHG-05 override endpoint
- ⚠️ HIPAA: scoring prompt must not request reproduction of meeting content — structured output only
Notes: Auto-scoring of the IPR meeting removes Nayab's manual Avoma link entry entirely.

Story Title: GitHub OIDC enforcement at MCP boundary
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- MCP server record_progress and notify_slack tools validate OIDC token on every call
- Token issuer must match expected GitHub Actions OIDC issuer (https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com) for CI calls, OR FusionAuth issuer for human/agent calls
- Invalid/missing token: tool call returns {written: false, reason: 'unauthorized'} — never throws unhandled error
- SSM parameter /deliverpro/config/oidc-enforcement: 'true'|'false' — when 'false', validation is logged but not enforced (migration mode)
- Enforcement mode switch logged to CloudWatch with actor sub when toggled
- Unit tests: valid CI token passes, missing token fails, wrong issuer fails, enforcement=false logs but passes
Notes: OIDC enforcement gated behind SSM flag — can be enabled incrementally.

Story Title: GitHub folder/structure enforcement gate check
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New Lambda: POST /api/projects/{id}/validate-structure
- Calls GitHub API to check presence of required paths in the linked github_repo: docs/project-progress.md, docs/ directory, .kiro/ directory
- Returns {valid: boolean, missing: string[], checked_at: timestamp}
- If github_repo is NULL: returns {valid: null, reason: 'no_github_repo_linked'}
- Result cached in projects.structure_last_validated_at TIMESTAMPTZ and projects.structure_valid BOOLEAN (V005f migration adds these columns)
- Gate status view (GET /api/projects/{id}/gates) includes structure_valid in Phase 0 data
- CloudWatch metric: StructureValidationFailed COUNT per project
Notes: Enforces .kiro folder and docs structure as a gate requirement.

Story Title: PMO read-only views — resource allocation + assignment dashboard
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/reporting/resource-allocation returns read-only view: for each active project, lists project_manager, solution_architect, implementation_lead, engineers_assigned, sow_hours, hours_consumed, burn_rate_pct, health_status
- No write path — leadership reads only
- Supports filter: pm, sa, status
- GET /api/reporting/person/{email} returns all projects where the person appears in any role field (pm, sa, implementation_lead, engineers_assigned contains their name)
- Response includes per-project: title, jira_key, current_phase, health_status, sow_hours, hours_consumed
- Auth: leadership, admin only
- Data is computed at query time from existing projects columns — no new tables
Notes: Read-only. No write paths. Auto-populated from existing data.

Story Title: PMO resource allocation UI
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New route /reports/resources (leadership/admin only)
- Table view: one row per active project, columns: project, PM, SA, implementation lead, engineers, SOW hours, consumed hours, burn rate %, RAG health
- Row click navigates to /projects/{id}
- Filter bar: by PM name, SA name, status
- 'My Projects' quick-filter for logged-in user: shows projects where current user email appears in any role
- Empty state handled; loading skeleton shown while fetching
- CASL: route hidden from non-leadership/admin
Notes: Chris's 'I want to see who is on what project' request.

Story Title: Non-5×5 project type templates — validate casdm_config is fully dynamic
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Existing casdm_config CRUD (DP-32) already supports project_type scoping; this story validates and seeds missing templates
- Seed V005g: POC project type template with reduced gate set (Phase 0 + Phase 1 + Phase 4 only; no IPR gate)
- Seed V005g: Migration project type template with Phase 0 as-built discovery gate added before Phase 1
- Seed V005g: Integration project type template matching migration template
- Each template seeded only if no rows exist for that project_type (idempotent)
- Integration test: POST /api/projects with project_type='POC' seeds the correct POC gate set
- GET /api/admin/config/project-types returns ['AppDev','AppMod','AIML','POC','Migration','Integration','Other','default']
Notes: Validates that casdm_config is truly dynamic, not 5×5-hardcoded.

Story Title: Agent Core / Strands migration — phase 1: SRS generation loop
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New Agent Core agent: deliverpro-srs-generator deployed in us-east-1
- Agent orchestrates: product-analyst → aws-architect review → product-analyst fix (max 3 loops) using Strands SDK
- Each loop step invokes the appropriate Bedrock model with the role's system prompt
- Loop exits when aws-architect response contains 'APPROVED' OR max iterations reached
- Final SRS written to S3 at srs/{projectId}/srs-v{n}.md; S3 key stored in projects.preliminary_srs_s3_key TEXT (V005h adds column)
- Trigger: POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-srs (manual trigger for now; Oscar auto-trigger deferred to Phase 4)
- Lambda timeout: 15 min; uses Step Functions for loop orchestration
- ⚠️ HIPAA: SRS generation prompt must not accept raw customer PHI as input — SOW summary only
Notes: Strands SDK loop. Oscar auto-feed deferred to Phase 4.

Story Title: Executive check-in views — auto-populated summary per phase
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/projects/{id}/exec-summary returns a read-only summary: current_phase, health_status, burn_rate_pct, open_escalations_count, open_decisions_count, last_status_log_date, next_milestone (next macro checkpoint with reached_at IS NULL)
- No new DB table — computed from existing columns at query time
- Frontend: collapsible Executive Summary card at the top of the project detail page
- Card shows: phase badge, RAG health dot, burn rate bar, open escalations count, open decisions count, last update date, next milestone name
- Visible to all roles; data is already accessible to them individually
- Refresh on every page load (no caching)
Notes: Surfaces what leadership already has access to in a single glance.

Story Title: Lessons-learned database — retrospective transcript scoring
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New table project_lessons: id UUID PK, project_id FK, lesson_type TEXT CHECK IN ('what_worked','what_didnt','improvement'), lesson_text TEXT NOT NULL, source TEXT CHECK IN ('retrospective','manual'), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_by TEXT (V005i migration)
- POST /api/projects/{id}/retrospective/score triggers Bedrock analysis of the retrospective meeting transcript (fetched from Avoma via ADD-09 pattern)
- Bedrock prompt extracts structured lessons: what worked, what didn't work, improvements
- Results written as project_lessons rows with source='retrospective'
- POST /api/projects/{id}/lessons allows manual lesson entry (source='manual')
- GET /api/projects/{id}/lessons returns all lessons grouped by lesson_type
- GET /api/reporting/lessons returns cross-project lessons (leadership/admin only) — queryable by type
- ⚠️ HIPAA: retrospective content handled same as Avoma — summary only in RDS
Notes: Enables Chris's 'lessons learned database from retrospectives' vision.

Story Title: Board/kanban multi-filter view — replicate Jira board
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- New view toggle on /projects route: List (current) and Board
- Board view shows columns: one column per CASDM phase (0-4) + Closed
- Project cards in each column show: title, jira_key, PM name, SA name, health_status dot, burn rate badge
- Column header shows count of projects in that phase
- Existing filters (status, PM, SA, type, search) apply to board view identically
- Selected view (list/board) persisted in localStorage
- Responsive: board collapses to list on mobile breakpoint
Notes: Frontend-only. Reuses existing GET /api/projects data.

Story Title: DEFERRED — Oscar integration + auto preliminary-SRS from presales context
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. Requires: Oscar API access, AgentCore SRS pipeline (ADD-16 partial), presales governance design, HIPAA review of SOW content handling.
- Phase 3 delivers manual SRS generation trigger (ADD-16); Oscar auto-feed waits for Phase 4.
Notes: Blocked on Oscar API, HIPAA review, and ADD-16 being stable.

Story Title: DEFERRED — FusionAuth OIDC migration (identity cutover)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. Own workstream. Must preserve: Cognito MFA, TLS 1.2+, access audit logging. Controlled cutover plan required.
- Phase 3 OIDC enforcement (ADD-11) uses GitHub Actions OIDC natively; FusionAuth is the human/agent path deferred here.
Notes: Do not bundle with feature work. Identity cutover requires its own release plan.

Story Title: DEFERRED — GitHub repo auto-creation on project init
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. Blocked on FusionAuth OIDC (DEF-02) being live.
- Phase 3 CHG-06 handles Slack channel auto-creation; GitHub repo creation waits.
Notes: GitHub App provisioning requires OIDC identity to be settled first.

Story Title: DEFERRED — Clockify integration (replace manual hours_consumed)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. Scheduled Lambda via Clockify API, keyed on Clockify project ID.
- Phase 3 keeps manual hours_consumed updates (DP-09).
Notes: New external system. Keep manual tracking through Phase 3.

Story Title: DEFERRED — SharePoint/Teams folder provisioning
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. Requires Microsoft Graph admin consent + app registration.
- Phase 3 Slack provisioning (CHG-06) is the only auto-provisioning delivered.
Notes: Microsoft Graph admin consent is a significant setup overhead.

Story Title: DEFERRED — Full project-intake/origination workflow (request → triage → create)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Deferred to Phase 4. request_type column ships in CHG-01 (Phase 3 V005).
- Full triage-to-create workflow (intake form, triage queue, AE assignment) waits for Phase 4.
Notes: request_type column lands in Phase 3 so the data is ready when Phase 4 builds the workflow.
